title: Redundant pointer operator sequences should be removed
description: Redundant pointer operator sequences should be removedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?By contract, chaining the 'Address of' operator&with the 'Indirection' operator*results in a return to the initial value. Thus, such combinations are confusing at best, and bugs at worst.Noncompliant code exampleint *ptr = ...; int *result1 = &(*ptr); //Noncompliant int *result2 = &*ptr; //Noncompliant int value = 4; int result3 = *(&value); //Noncompliant int result4 = *&value; //NoncompliantCompliant solutionint *ptr = ...; int *result1 = ptr; int *result2 = ptr; int value = 4; int result3 = value; int result4 = value;ExceptionsNo issue is raised when the*or&operators are overloaded or when both operators are not located in the same piece of c
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Redundant pointer operator sequences detected - & and * operators cancel each other out
Redundant address-of and indirection operators should be removed for clarity
Confusing pointer operator sequence creates unnecessary complexity and potential bugs
Redundant pointer operators found in assignment, function call, or sizeof expression
